changes_summary: The plan stays on the same root cause and code path, but it is now explicit about the repository's real invariants: selection identity must preserve `variant_id`, queued tasks must persist `timeline_id` and anchor metadata, and `complete_task` must place the exact completed asset ref (`generation_id` plus `variant_id` when applicable), not just a generation. The revision also removes scope creep by limiting v1 automatic placement to single selected timeline clips from the editor-chat flow.

success_criteria.criterion: A selected timeline asset can be resolved server-side to structured context including `timeline_id`, `clip_id`, `generation_id`, and `variant_id` without relying on prompt-text matching.

A source-derived editor-chat task persists both generation lineage (`based_on`) and exact source variant lineage (`source_variant_id`) plus enough placement intent to recover the target timeline and anchor at completion time.

Completion-time placement uses the exact completed asset identity, including `variant_id` when the output is a new variant on an existing generation, and inserts it on the persisted timeline after the anchor clip's live end time.
Image-to-image and magic-edit remain variants by default unless the user explicitly asks for a detached or standalone result.
Moved-anchor and deleted-anchor behavior is explicit, deterministic, and covered by targeted tests.
Focused tests for attachment forwarding, selected-clip normalization, task persistence, and `complete_task` placement pass in the touched suites.

assumptions: This restored plan continues the original deleted workstream and is reconstructed from surviving logs.
The first implementation targets tasks created from the editor-chat `ai-timeline-agent` flow, not every task producer in the app.

V1 automatic placement is limited to exactly one selected timeline clip in the active session timeline; gallery-only selections may still drive generation but do not create placement intent by default.

The default placement mode is non-ripple insertion on the anchor clip's track at the anchor clip's current end time, with fallback to the stored absolute snapshot only when the anchor clip has been deleted.

For v1, task-scoped placement metadata can live inside `tasks.params` and completion follow-up state can live in existing task result/follow-up fields without requiring a broader task-schema redesign.
